Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of (S)-coclaurine, supporting upstream BIA flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a candidate 6OMT in A. manshuriensis based on phylogeny, catalytic residues, and expression. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana showed strong catalytic conversion of (S)-norcoclaurine to (S)-coclaurine. 3. Functions in the shared-upstream pathway feeding BIA and aristolochic acid I biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; conserved residue analysis; expression pattern analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; substrate feeding with 1 mM (S)-norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman4, a well-conserved chromosomal block derived from ancestral AMK7; stronger activity than paralog Am04G071000
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing or enhancing upstream BIA flux in heterologous systems; can be stacked with CNMT and NMCH for pathway engineering
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential accumulation of toxic alkaloid intermediates if engineered

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a paralogous candidate 6OMT in A. manshuriensis. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana showed weak conversion of (S)-norcoclaurine to (S)-coclaurine. 3. Likely derived from recent WGD and may have undergone subfunctionalization relative to 4'OMT paralogous relationships in the lineage.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; conserved residue analysis; expression pattern analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; substrate feeding with 1 mM (S)-norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman4; paired by WGD history with Am02G209000 in evolutionary analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary gene for BIA engineering, though lower catalytic efficiency suggests less value than Am04G071300

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to (S)-coclaurine to generate (S)-N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of (S)-N-methylcoclaurine and supports BIA production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among two CNMT candidates, Am05G021710 was experimentally validated as the active CNMT. 2. It catalyzes conversion of (S)-coclaurine to (S)-N-methylcoclaurine in transient assays. 3. Evolutionary analysis showed the WGD-derived paralog Am03G024800 lost the CMAS domain, while Am05G021710 retained CNMT function.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; protein structural comparison; conserved catalytic determinant analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; substrate feeding with 1 mM (S)-coclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shares highly similar structure with TfCNMT (RMSD 0.531 Å); located on Aman5 retained from ancestral AMK10
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for reconstructing core BIA pathway and testing metabolic routes to aristolochic acid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may elevate toxic alkaloid intermediates

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates (S)-N-methylcoclaurine at the 3' position to form (S)-3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of hydroxylated intermediate in core BIA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Am04G071200 was identified as the candidate CYP80B/NMCH gene in A. manshuriensis. 2. Transient expression confirmed conversion of (S)-N-methylcoclaurine to (S)-3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine. 3. It occupies a conserved BIA-gene syntenic block retained in magnoliids and early-diverging eudicots.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; conserved residue analysis; expression pattern analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; substrate feeding with 1 mM (S)-N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman4 retained from ancestral AMK7; CYP80B/NMCH branch preferentially retained in magnoliids and early-diverging eudicots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core hydroxylase for assembling heterologous BIA pathway modules
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential production of toxic downstream alkaloids

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to participate in BIA intermediate methylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the candidate 4'OMT based on phylogeny, catalytic residues, and expression patterns. 2. Evolutionary analysis indicates it arose from WGD relative to Am04G071000 and likely subfunctionalized. 3. No catalytic activity was detected in the transient expression system for tested 4'OMT genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; conserved residue analysis; expression pattern analysis; transient expression test with no detectable activity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in N. benthamiana tested
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman2 in a conserved BIA-associated block derived from ancestral AMK7
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for completing alternative or flexible methylation orders in magnoliid BIA engineering, but function requires further validation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function uncertain; no activity observed in assay

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate selected based on expression patterns in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted contributor to BIA intermediate modification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a candidate 7OMT in A. manshuriensis based on phylogeny, catalytic residues, and expression. 2. Retained from ancestral AMK1 in a conserved chromosomal block. 3. Supports the inferred diversification of methylation steps in magnoliid BIA pathways, though direct enzymatic validation was not reported.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; conserved residue analysis; expression pattern analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct transient activity result reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate accessory methyltransferase for reconstructing magnoliid-specific BIA diversity

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: CYP80G2-type intramolecular C-C phenol coupling enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted intramolecular C-C coupling in aporphine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Aporphine alkaloids; aristolochic acid I-related pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to support aporphine branch formation from BIA intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a candidate CYP80G2 gene for intramolecular C-C coupling. 2. Proposed to function in the aporphine alkaloid branch downstream of shared BIA intermediates. 3. Evolutionary analysis indicates magnoliids retained CYP80G2 orthologs, unlike some other BIA-producing lineages.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential branch-enzyme target for engineering aporphine-type alkaloid diversity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unvalidated function; may channel flux toward toxic products

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: CYP80Q-type intermolecular C-O phenol coupling enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted intermolecular C-O coupling in bisbenzylisoquinoline/proaporphine alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Bisbenzylisoquinoline/proaporphine alkaloids; aristolochic acid I-related pathway intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to support branch diversification of BIA-derived alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a candidate CYP80Q gene for intermolecular C-O coupling. 2. Proposed to participate in bisbenzylisoquinoline/proaporphine alkaloid biosynthesis. 3. Magnoliids retained CYP80Q orthologs, contributing to broader BIA pathway diversity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate branch enzyme for engineering magnoliid-specific alkaloid scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unvalidated function; may increase toxic alkaloid profiles

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted to introduce a methylenedioxy bridge onto aporphine alkaloid substrates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Aporphine alkaloids; aristolochic acid I-related metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidate genes identified in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to decorate downstream aporphine-type intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Five candidate CYP719 genes were identified in A. manshuriensis. 2. These are proposed to introduce the methylenedioxy bridge on aporphine alkaloid substrates. 3. CYP719 family expansion was highlighted at the magnoliid ancestor level, consistent with enriched alkaloid biosynthesis potential.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific gene IDs and direct enzyme assays were not provided in the Results text excerpt
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tailoring enzymes for generating structural diversity in BIA-derived metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unresolved substrate specificity; downstream toxic products possible

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: NCS genes (14 candidates including Am05G154100, Am05G154200, Am05G155000)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted formation of (S)-norcoclaurine at the entry to BIA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized defense-related and medicinal/toxic alkaloid metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Candidates identified based on phylogeny and expression; Am05G154200 noted on Aman5
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tested in transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted entry-point enzyme for BIA formation, but no catalytic activity was detected for tested candidates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fourteen NCS candidates were identified in A. manshuriensis, belonging to the Bet v1 subfamily. 2. Most functional NCS genes across species fall in the NCS-I clade, retained mainly in magnoliids and early-diverging eudicots. 3. None of the tested A. manshuriensis NCS genes showed catalytic activity in the N. benthamiana system. 4. A pair of NCS-I genes, Am05G154100 and Am05G155000, are flanked by Copia elements, suggesting TE-mediated duplication.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; expression pattern analysis; transient expression test with no detectable activity; TE-flanking analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core to the paper’s BIA pathway investigation despite failed functional validation in assay; likely important evolutionary retained entry-step genes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for future validation to enable full pathway reconstruction from entry step onward
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function unresolved in current assay system
